articles.title: Emergency services respond to incident in Middleton town centre
articles.excerpt: Emergency services attended an incident in Middleton town centre involving a man on a roof, leading to a significant police presence in the area.
articles.content_html:
Emergency services responded to an incident in Middleton town centre on Tuesday afternoon, involving a man on a roof. Police and other emergency services arrived at the scene, creating a significant presence in the area.

Witnesses reported seeing emergency vehicles and personnel surrounding the location as efforts were made to resolve the situation. The nature of the incident has not been disclosed, and no injuries have been reported.

Authorities have cordoned off the area while they manage the situation. Local residents and businesses may experience disruptions as a result of the ongoing response.

Further updates will be provided as more information becomes available.

articles.title: Rochdale Heritage Project Shortlisted for National Archaeology Award
articles.excerpt: A heritage project in Rochdale has been shortlisted for a prestigious national archaeology award, recognising its contribution to local history and culture.
articles.content_html:
A heritage project in Rochdale has been shortlisted for a prestigious national archaeology award. The project aims to highlight the area's rich historical significance and engage the community in its preservation.

The award recognises innovative approaches to archaeology and heritage management, with the shortlisted projects demonstrating excellence in research and public engagement.

Details about the specific contributions of the Rochdale project have not been disclosed, but it is expected to draw attention to the local history and archaeological findings in the region.

The announcement of the award winners is anticipated later this year, which could further elevate the profile of Rochdale's historical initiatives.

articles.title: Croft Shifa Pharmacy in Rochdale Sold to First-Time Buyer
articles.excerpt: Croft Shifa Pharmacy, located on Belfield Road, has been sold to first-time buyer Hossiendad Hossieni, marking a significant change in ownership for the local health centre pharmacy.
articles.content_html:
Croft Shifa Pharmacy, located on Belfield Road, has been sold to a first-time buyer following a deal completed by specialist advisers. The pharmacy dispenses an average of 6,400 items each month and operates as a standard-hours health centre pharmacy.

The new owner, Hossiendad Hossieni of NHSR Limited, recognised the potential of the pharmacy, which has been run under full management for some time. Tom Young, senior business agent at Christie & Co, who handled the sale, expressed confidence in Hossieni's ability to grow the business further.

Young noted that Greater Manchester remains a hub for pharmacy transactions, with high demand from various buyer types. He stated that Croft Shifa Pharmacy presented a great opportunity for a first-time buyer and operator to enter a pharmacy that has been well-managed.

The sale is expected to enhance the pharmacy's integration with the health centre, as Hossieni aims to improve the capture rate from the facility. Young wished Hossieni the best for the future, stating he has no doubt Hossieni will take the business to the next level.

articles.title: Middleton McDonald's Reopens Following Major Redesign
articles.excerpt: McDonald's in Middleton has reopened after a significant refurbishment aimed at enhancing customer and staff experiences, featuring updated technology and improved facilities.
articles.content_html:
A McDonald's restaurant in Middleton has reopened following a major refurbishment designed to enhance the experience for both customers and staff. The redesign includes a new layout, updated technology, and separate facilities for delivery couriers.

The restaurant, owned by local franchisee Gerald Thompson, has undergone changes as part of McDonald's latest reimagining programme. This initiative aims to improve speed, efficiency, and choice for customers. The refurbishment features a redesigned kitchen and dining area that supports digital ordering through the McDonald's app, allowing customers to order in advance and either collect their food or have it delivered to their table upon arrival.

The front counter has been restructured to create dedicated areas for different ordering methods, which McDonald's states will reduce congestion and shorten queues around self-service ordering screens. Additionally, improvements have been made behind the scenes, including a larger order assembly area and dedicated preparation spaces for dine-in meals.

Gerald Thompson, who operates 18 McDonald's restaurants across Greater Manchester, expressed pride in reinvesting in the Middleton location, which he first opened as a franchisee in 2000. He noted that the redesign provides a fresh look and enhances the experience for both customers and the restaurant's team of over eighty staff members.

articles.title: Rochdale Pub Named Among Best in the UK by The Telegraph
articles.excerpt: The Baum in Rochdale has been included in The Telegraph's annual list of the best pubs in the UK for 2026, recognised for its food and welcoming atmosphere.
articles.content_html:
A pub in Rochdale has been named among the best in the UK. The Baum, located on Toad Lane, features in The Telegraph's annual list of The Best Pubs in the UK for 2026, which includes 500 pubs from England, 75 from Scotland, 50 from Wales, and 25 from Northern Ireland.

The selection process involved a panel of judges who assessed each pub based on criteria such as character, staff friendliness, and historical significance. Nineteen pubs from Greater Manchester made the list, with The Baum being highlighted for its food offerings, including traditional Bury black pudding served in a scotch egg and on the breakfast menu.

Although The Baum is not a historic pub, having been converted from a hardware shop in the 1980s, it has gained a reputation for its welcoming atmosphere and diverse menu. The pub offers a range of lunch and evening options, featuring classic dishes like fish and chips, as well as smaller plates such as seafood tacos and lamb kofta tacos.

The recognition from The Telegraph is likely to attract visitors from nearby areas, including Bury, who may be interested in experiencing the pub's acclaimed food and inviting environment.
